characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: A robot boy built to replace his creator's deceased son. He possesses the strength of 100,000 horsepower (later 1,000,000) and a heart of gold. He possesses what's referred to as the Omega Factor / Kokoro, which allows him to feel emotions and thought processes identical to that of humans (and in the 2003 anime, tomodify his body as he grows mentally). His ultimate wish is to create a world where humans and robots live and work as equals — sort of like a tin-can Martin Luther King Jr. His powers include super-strength, rocket-propelled flight, universal translation, super senses, a laser finger/arm cannon, and twin machine guns in his butt.Clickhereto see Atom from theUltimate UniverseofPluto, andhereto seeA106. All-Loving Hero: He firmly believes that humans and A.I robots can coexist peacefully as equals. Whenever possible, he tries to talk his enemies down instead of blasting them to ashes.Ultimately forgives the Big Bad for all his evil and this causes his final defeat. Arm Cannon: Gained it with the 2003 series in his left arm, wherein it became and still remains his most powerful weapon of all.noteInherited this from Jetter Mars. Ass Kicks You: As mentioned already, he hasmachine guns in his butt.noteoriginally they were lasers, but this was ditched later down the road Badass Adorable: Given the fact that he's an incredibly powerful combat robot that resembles a small boy, it's not a big surprise.

characters.name: Atom/Astro Boy
characters.description: A robot boy built to replace his creator's deceased son. He possesses the strength of 100,000 horsepower (later 1,000,000) and a heart of gold. He possesses what's referred to as the Omega Factor / Kokoro, which allows him to feel emotions and thought processes identical to that of humans (and in the 2003 anime, tomodify his body as he grows mentally). His ultimate wish is to create a world where humans and robots live and work as equals — sort of like a tin-can Martin Luther King Jr. His powers include super-strength, rocket-propelled flight, universal translation, super senses, a laser finger/arm cannon, and twin machine guns in his butt.Clickhereto see Atom from theUltimate UniverseofPluto, andhereto seeA106. All-Loving Hero: He firmly believes that humans and A.I robots can coexist peacefully as equals. Whenever possible, he tries to talk his enemies down instead of blasting them to ashes.Ultimately forgives the Big Bad for all his evil and this causes his final defeat. Arm Cannon: Gained it with the 2003 series in his left arm, wherein it became and still remains his most powerful weapon of all.noteInherited this from Jetter Mars. Ass Kicks You: As mentioned already, he hasmachine guns in his butt.noteoriginally they were lasers, but this was ditched later down the road Badass Adorable: Given the fact that he's an incredibly powerful combat robot that resembles a small boy, it's not a big surprise.

characters.name: Uran/Astro Girl/Zoran
characters.description: Astro's "sister". Much brattier than her brother, she has fewer compunctions about using her abilities for personal gain and sometimes shows less regard for human life, or at least human values as well, which would be kind of scary if she weren't so darned cute. Though she is less interested in humans than her brother, she has a fondness for other living things, and in some versions is able to communicate with animals. In her debut story she also gained the power to split herself in two, but due to her strength being halved, it is never used again. Uran's origins are somewhat vague, as Tezuka sometimes stated she was created by Ochanomizu, and at other times by Tenma as a failed prototype.Clickhereto see Uran from theUltimate UniverseofPluto, andhereto seeA107. Big Brother Worship: Although Uran can be really annoying and rude to Astro at times, she still looks up to her older brother and wants to be as strong as he is. Bratty Half-Pint: In the eighties series, she causes no small amount of trouble for Astro and others, whether it be bullying kids into giving her their toys, causing massive amounts of property damage because she wants to catch a cockroach, or talking back to people who scold her for just about anything. It somewhat continues in the 2003 anime,but she is definitely much more calm in this version. Deliberately Cute Child: In the eighties series, she's very well aware of how cute she is and often tries to use her looks to disarm people into giving her what she wants regardless of her actual behavior. Distaff Counterpart: Obviously one to Astro, being his "sister".

characters.name: Cobalt/Jetto
characters.description: Astro's younger brother. He is omitted from most adaptations of the series, but can be seen in the original manga and the 1960's anime. In the manga, he was originally created to replace Astro - who'd gone missing - during a crisis. When the crisis ended and both robots met each other, it was decided that they would live as brothers together. As Cobalt was not created by Tenma, he lacks the power and capabilities that Astro has. While similar in appearance, Cobalt is taller than Astro and his hair spikes are longer. Adapted Out: Cobalt has seemingly been lost to the franchise's history. Following the original manga and 1963 animated adaptation, futureAstro Boyproductions would drop him entirely, often transplanting his story, role, personality or themes into other characters.

characters.name: Astro's Parents (Rin and Ethanol) / Mama & Papa / Astro's Mom and Dad
characters.description: Adaptational Attractiveness:While already having a pretty design, Rin is given a more attractive design in the 1980s series.InThe Original Astro Boy, Rin's given an even more attractive appearance resembling a typical American housewife during the 80s.InLittle Astro BoyRin and Ethanol are given notably cuter designs alongside closely resembling Astro's appearance. They even have a similar hair style as their son. While already having a pretty design, Rin is given a more attractive design in the 1980s series. InThe Original Astro Boy, Rin's given an even more attractive appearance resembling a typical American housewife during the 80s. InLittle Astro BoyRin and Ethanol are given notably cuter designs alongside closely resembling Astro's appearance. They even have a similar hair style as their son. Adaptational Backstory Change: In the manga and other incarnations, they were created by Professor Ochanomizu so Astro won't feel lonely. In the 1980s anime, Astro discovers Rin and Ethanol as two unprogrammed robots in a middle of a mission. After getting rescued, Ochanomizu decides to turn them into Astro's robotic parents.

characters.name: The Blue Knight
characters.description: Adaptational Backstory Change: In the manga he is part of a small family of robots made by his creator, and becomes filled with rage after his sister and brother were killed by his sister's human suitor. In the 2003 series he's a worker assigned to repair those damaged in the aftermath of illegal robot fighting matches of Hammegg until he had enough of his master's carelessness of his underlings, trying to escape with the rest of the slaves until he was captured by Hammegg and ejected from the spaceship they were in, later being saved by Shadow and turning him into the Blue Knight. Adaptational Nice Guy: Is less aggressive and disdainful in the 2003 series than in the manga; he doesn't talk down on or speak ill of human-following robots, and warns Astro several times about what will happen if he interferes before finally deciding to fight him. Adaptational Relationship Overhaul: Dr. Los is omitted from the 2003 adaptation as Blue Bon's creator, leaving the closest thing to his "father" being Shadow, who is made into the one that turns him into the Blue Knight. Air Jousting: His usual way of fighting, done astride a flying robot horse. Anti-Hero/Anti-Villain: Genuinely wants what's best for robotkind, but goes to great extremes to see it happen. Whether it be to attacks on human settlements or making his fellow robots to get aggressive on the humans, the Blue Knightwillsee robotkind free from humans.

characters.name: Shadow
characters.description: Adaptational Villainy: In the 2003 series, Shadow betrays Dr. Tenma and leaves the Earth peacefully. In the Astro Boy Videogame, Shadow betrays Tenma, kills Pluto using anEvil Counterpartnamed Acheron he created, and then tries to kill off humanity and Earth itself to rule over a world of robots.He's evenworseinAstro Boy: Omega Factor,as there he's the leader of an anti-human robot army in aBad Futurewho's reallySharaku. He's evenworseinAstro Boy: Omega Factor,as there he's the leader of an anti-human robot army in aBad Futurewho's reallySharaku. A.I. Is a Crapshoot:A benevolent and apathetic version in the 2003 series where Shadow decides he's had enough of Dr. Tenma's obsession with Astro and takes on his appearance, betrays him, and decides to lead Robotonia with the Blue Knight into space to avoid humanity and create a new peaceful world of robots. Composite Character: Shadow is a bit of a mix-up of Dr. Abullah/Dr. Goji from theStrongest Robot In The Worldarc, being a mysterious robot roboticist with a shroud who made one of the strongest characters in the Astro Boy series.InAstro Boy: Omega Factor, he's a robot disguise for Sharaku. InAstro Boy: Omega Factor, he's a robot disguise for Sharaku.

characters.name: Atlas
characters.description: Adaptation Species Change: Or "Dub-species change." Atlas in the 2003 anime was a robot modeled after the son of a wealthy businessman who died tragically, in a way that is clearly meant to mirror Tobio. The 2003 anime's dub instead indicates that he was a robot from the beginning, and his father forcefully deactivated him after stepping out of line one time too many. Instead of demanding Tenma rebuild his son as a robot, Atlas's father in the dub demands, then later begs in desperation (clearly a holdover from the original of him grieving over is son's death) for Tenma to fix him. The end result is the same. Ascended Extra: Atlas was originally a one-off villain in the originalAstro Boyseries, but later adaptations would promote him to a much higher standing. The 1980s animated adaptation makes him an overarching villain, while the 2003 adaptation makes him a lesser recurring character. Back for the Finale:Shadow had revived him to become one of the top soldiers of Robotonia in the 2003 adaptation and defend their citadel from the invading humans. Cain and Abel: In a sense, he is the Cain to Atom's Abel. They are portrated as brothers in a sense thanks to Atlas being created either by the same blueprints that Atom was made with or he is also built by Tenma himself. And while Atom is shown to be nothing short of a sweet and caring child, Atlas is theRobot Kidequivalent of anEnfant Terrible. Composite Character: Both colour anime series' combine him with Cobalt, Astro's brother from the original series. The 80s version also adds aspects of The Blue Knight, but this wasn't repeated in the later series, where Blue Knight was retained as a separate character.

characters.name: Zero/Electro/Denkou
characters.description: Cheerful Child: He is as cheerful and lively as a real human child. Children Are Innocent: Because of his child-like innocense, Skunk used his ability to turn invisible to make him steal valuables while tricking Denkou that this is all a good thing. The moment he stops being useful to the criminal, he leaves the child who is left wondering why he would do such a thing. Team Member in the Adaptation: InAstro Boy: Omega Factor,he's one of the World's Strongest Robots.

characters.name: Dr. Umataro Tenma / Dr. Boynton / Dr. Balthus
characters.description: Abusive Parents:Tenma is an earlymangaexample. After he lost his son in a traffic accident, he built Astro Boy as aReplacement Goldfish. Shortly afterwards, he realized a Replacement Goldfish is not a substitute for the original thing, and he began to berate and scorn Astro constantly, yelling him for such a nefarious crimes like being unable to grow up. Finally he got fed up with him and sold him in slavery. Thank God that he was programmed to be a "good boy" and was taken in by Dr. Ochanomizu, who was an altruistic, kind man instilled and nurtured those traits in Astro Boy, or maybe he could have become aWoobie, Destroyer of Worlds.He was never there for Tobio and constantly neglected him in favor of working at the Ministry of Science. Even when he cried for his dead son, instead of mourning it like any normal human being, Tenma decided toreplacehis dead son with a robot copy, becoming an even worser father than before, getting angry at him for something as obvious as a robot not being able to grow like a real kid, selling him out to Hamegg later on. Tenma is an earlymangaexample. After he lost his son in a traffic accident, he built Astro Boy as aReplacement Goldfish. Shortly afterwards, he realized a Replacement Goldfish is not a substitute for the original thing, and he began to berate and scorn Astro constantly, yelling him for such a nefarious crimes like being unable to grow up. Finally he got fed up with him and sold him in slavery. Thank God that he was programmed to be a "good boy" and was taken in by Dr. Ochanomizu, who was an altruistic, kind man instilled and nurtured those traits in Astro Boy, or maybe he could have become aWoobie, Destroyer of Worlds. He was never there for Tobio and constantly neglected him in favor of working at the Ministry of Science. Even when he cried for his dead son, instead of mourning it like any normal human being, Tenma decided toreplacehis dead son with a robot copy, becoming an even worser father than before, getting an

characters.name: Professor Ochanomizu / Dr. Packerdermus J. Elefun / Dr. O'Shay
characters.description: Absent-Minded Professor: He's a genius surpassed by very few, but he also would've been lost without his secretary, robot maid, etc. reminding him not to forget meetings, not to come too late to office, not to neglect his duties as Minister of Science, etc. Adaptational Intelligence: The 2003 series has him reactivate Astro without having any safeguards in place to keep an all-powerful robot child from causing damage. In the 2009 film, he questions Tenma's wisdom in recreating Tobio and tries to help Astro when the boy realizes he's not human. Berserk Button: He doesn't like it when people make fun of his nose. In the 1960s series, he would start sneezing when getting angry. Beware the Nice Ones: He is generally peaceful and preaches about peace between robotkind and humanity.His peacefulness flies out from the window when he witnesses the brutal death of the Blue Knight at the hands of Count Burg, enraging him so much that hepersonally beats him up and burries him to the ground with his nose no less!◊ Big Good: In stark contrast to Dr. Tenma, he believes mankind and robotkind can live together in harmony to build a better future for both; it was this kindness that inspired Astro to become the hero he is.

characters.name: Shunsaku Ban / Albert Duncan / Hector / Percival Pompous / Mr. Mustachio / Higeoyaji / Daddy Walrus
characters.description: Black Bead Eyes: His default design depicted him with beady eyes, but is sometimes given pupils in some of his other appearances alongside the 1980s and 2003 incarnations. Cool Old Guy: An old man who despite his eccentricities and moments, is a well meaning detective and overall a good man. Expansion Pack Past: Besides being Astro's homeroom teacher in the first two anime series, he is also a private detective, a judo master, and a florist, among others. Jerk with a Heart of Gold: As mentioned before, he's gruff and short-tempered, but he LOVES his job as a teacher to children and Astro.

characters.name: Police Inspector Tawashi / Inspector Gumshoe
characters.description: A detective who is distrustful of robots and androids. Due to his position and opinions, he frequently butts heads with Ochanomizu and Astro because his style of work when it comes to robot criminals is to shoot first and ask questions later. He eventually comes around to respect and appreciate Astro Boy. Often forms half of aGood Cop/Bad Copact with fellow recurring policeman Inspector Nakamura. Badass Longcoat: Wears one of this in the 2003 series, and its not for show. Composite Character: In the 2003 series, Nakamura’s competency and more benevolent attributes have been given to Tawashi. As such Tawashi is both a far more competent cop and afar nicer one. The Commissioner Gordon: He often assists Astro Boy in fighting crime, even if he is very groutchy about it. Da Chief: Eternally gruff and grumpy, he is constantly angry.

characters.name: Hamegg / The Great Katchitori
characters.description: Adaptational Job Change: His 2003 incarnation has no connection to the circus like all incarnations prior, instead running an illegal robot fighting ring. Cheshire Cat Grin: Hamegg'sdefault expression, even when angered or distressed he keeps showing his teeth. Bait the Dog: Hamegg gets a case ofAdaptational Heroismfor almost half of the 2009 film and he genuinely treats the children he cares for with care and respect, even comforting Astro on his first night about being an "orphan"...Then he puts on his classic top hat and knocks out Astro for his fighting games. Later, he tries tocallously abandon all of said childrenso that he can go get rich somewhere else. Fortunately, Zog holds him while the kids grab his car. Bitch in Sheep's Clothing:In the '80s anime he pulls anAffably Evilstunt on Astro by taking advantage of him being hurt by the scolding he received from his father, and talks him intosigning a contractto work in his robot circus, which turns out to be a trick in order for Hamegg to have complete ownership of him and legally protects him from Prof. Ochanomizu wanting to take Astro away from him.In the2009 film, where he appears to care for orphaned children and robots, until it's revealed that he... really doesn't care about robotsnorthe orphaned children; he just cares about money. He also puts robots (including Astro) in gladiator fightsjust for self-entertainment. In the '80s anime he pulls anAffably Evilstunt on Astro by taking advantage of him being hurt by the scolding he received from his father, and talks him intosigning a contractto work in his robot circus, which turns out to be a trick in order for Hamegg to have complete ownership of him and legally protects him from Prof. Ochanomizu wanting to take Astro away from him.

characters.name: Skunk Kusai / Fearless Fred Fenk
characters.description: Adaptational Badass: He is often portrayed as your average dumbass thief who gets himself defeated by Atom all the time. In the 2003 series, he is far more competent and intelligent than before. Blue Means Cold: Before he had normal-looking skin in the 2003 series, Skunk was blue in the 1980 series. Even by looking at the color of his skin gives you the chills. The Chessmaster: In the 2003 series. Healwayshas a backup plan. Dirty Coward: He never acts on honor, but for his own pride. Expect him to escape when things get dire for him. Lean and Mean: A lanky, thin gangster who relishes in puppeteering robots for personal gain.

characters.name: Kenichi / Kennedy
characters.description: Race Lift: The 2003 series made himAmbiguously Brown Those Two Guys: Him and Tamao, though mostly averted in the 2003 series.

characters.name: Tamao / Alejo
characters.description: Signature Headgear: He rarely shows up without his trademark cap, which was made cooler in the 2003 series. Those Two Guys: Him and Kenichi, though mostly averted in the 2003 series.

characters.name: Shibugaki / Abercrombie
characters.description: Adaptational Nice Guy: He is less of a big bully in the 2003 series. He was initially hostile to Astro when the latter joins class, but quickly warms up to him after Astro saves him from falling. Too Dumb to Live: In the 1980s series, his selfishness and recklessness often end up getting him and his friends in life-or-death situations. Despite his anti-robot sentiments, if Astro Boy had never shown up to save his life countless times throughout the series, he'd have been dead as early as the first episode.

characters.name: Tobio / Toby
characters.description: Bloodless Carnage: When his corpse was shownfor a few secondsin the 2003 anime, no external injuries or blood was shown, even though he was killed in a car accident. Brilliant, but Lazy: In the 2009 movie, Toby is established as being really smart. He can finish a physics quiz in a matter of seconds. Toby, however, would rather leave school early to spend time with his dad. Tenma tells him to not get complacent. Drives Like Crazy: Deconstructed in most versions; he takes a robot car to drive home after a fight with his father and keeps swerving. Then Tenma reveals thatkids can't drive robot carswhen realizing his son took off. This leads to him getting killed when he runs into a truck. Hates Their Parent: The 2003 version of Tobio outright yelled at Tenma that he hated him for both neglecting him for years and for trying to convince him to leave Robita (the only being that he considered a friend during all that time) behind and be replaced with a newer model. I Don't Want to Die: The 2009 version has a realistic case of this: he spends his last moments scared and calling for help while trapped behind a barrier with thePeacekeeper. He begs his dad to rescue him, as Tenma desperately tries to free him and can only reassure his son in his last moments.

characters.name: Pluto/Bruton
characters.description: A giant monster of a machine with the sole purpose of hunting down and killing all the world's most powerful robots, a job which he has no particular enthusiasm for, but feels is the only thing he is capable of doing. Originally this was simply so that his selfish owner could boast that he had the greatest robot on Earth, but later versions usually assign deeper and more sinister motives for his killing spree.He is also thetitle characterof anUltimate Universeremake byNaoki Urasawa, acclaimed creator ofMonster (1994)and20th Century Boys. Adaptational Heroism: InAstro Boy: Omega Factor,he may be part of a league of robots who are fighting against humans, but he's also fighting for more sympathetic reasons than any other incarnation of the character and is friends with Zoran. Affably Evil: When Pluto says it's not personal, it'snot personal. He has no concept of hatred and seeminglycan'thold a personal grudge. Honestly, if you're not one of his targets or a means to get at them, he can actually be rather pleasant. Just ask Uran. Erm, before he knew who she was, of course. Animal Motifs: Beetles. His shiny and smooth dark colored armor with spikes on shoulders and large horns brings to mind rhinoceros and stag beetles. It's also fitting given his immense strength, as rhinoceros and stag beetles are known to be quite strong insects that can lift things several times their size. His 2003. version has segmented arms identical to those of some insects. Back from the Dead:In the 2000s series, by machinations of Shadow and the Blue Knight, he is rebuilt back to act as a elite fighter for Robotonia to fend off the human invaders and just in time for the series' conclusion.

characters.name: Mont Blanc
characters.description: Curb-Stomp Battle: Part of it is probably due to Mont Blanc being caught off-guard by Pluto suddenly attacking him, but he's utterlydecimatedonce Pluto confirms his identity. Demoted to Extra: In the 2003 anime, Mont Blanc is turned into a nameless robot under Hamegg's order to fight Pluto and give everyone a show. He gets killed almost instantaniously. Gentle Giant: Despite his large build and incredible strength of many horsepower, he's rather friendly and gentle and seems content to just be a mountain guide. Nature-Loving Robot: He works as a gentle lumberjack who knows what treets to cut and respects the nature of the mountains, acting as a mountain guide in Switzerland. Sacrificial Lamb: Probably the most gentle-natured of the world's top robots, and he's the first robot Pluto eliminates, proving just how dangerous Pluto is.

characters.name: North#2/Monar
characters.description: Demoted to Extra: North #2 gets turned into a nameless mook in the 2003 anime to fight Pluto and make Hamegg lots of money by broadcasting it. Multi-Armed and Dangerous: Has six retractable arms perfect for fighting with. Even if they're torn off, he also has a bunch of drills and claws hidden inside him to replace them. Robot Butler: North #2 was built and works for a Scottish scientist both as a butler and amongst the most powerful robots in the world.

characters.name: Brando
characters.description: Acrofatic: As expected of a wrestler robot; he's much more heavyset than other robots, but can still throw his weight around. Demoted to Extra: He, North #2 and Mont Blanc get turned into nameless fighting robots to fight Pluto under Hamegg's orders. They all get killed in10 seconds. Roaring Rampage of Revenge: He hunts down Pluto to avenge Mont Blanc, who was a good friend of his, but Pluto kills him instead. Robot Athlete: He makes a living as a wrestler robot in Turkey.

characters.name: Gerhardt/Gesicht
characters.description: Chest Blaster: He has a set of guns on his chest and takes off his shirt to properly use them. Gold-Colored Superiority: Downplayed; His Zeronium body gives him a golden hue and also makes him immune to Pluto'sOne-Hit Killtechnique, but it's not enough to get him to victory. Half the Man He Used to Be: Pluto bends his horns apart while Gerhardt is gripping onto them, causing him to be torn in half. No-Sell: The alloy he's made of makes him immune to Pluto'selectromagnetic ray blast.Averted otherwise, as Pluto still manages to kill him.

characters.name: Hercules
characters.description: Back for the Finale:He is brought back in the 2003 series thanks to Shadow's actions, who revived him to act as an elite soldier for Robotonia and defend it from the humans. Honor Before Reason: Epsilon arranges a meeting with him to discuss joining forces to defeat Pluto, and indeed the two's combined might probably might have been able to defeat him, but unfortunately Hercules is just too prideful in his abilities and honor as a warrior to listen.

characters.name: Epsilon/Photar
characters.description: A photon-powered robot from Australia and one of the seven most powerful robots in the world that Pluto targeted. Pluto arranges for a fight with him in the rain, preventing him from using the sun's photons for power and making him an easy victory. He's the sixth robot Pluto kills. Friend to All Children: It doesn't matter what incarnation it is, Epsilon always has some kids he takes care of Friend to All Living Things: In both incarnations, Epsilon has consistently been shown as the most compassionate super-robot to both kids and the wildlife. Gender Flip: Originally a male. In the 2003 series, Epsilon is shown to be a female instead. Leotard of Power: The 2003 incarnation spots a dark green leotard over a purple bodysuit.

characters.name: Bora
characters.description: Flat Character: Bora has no features other than being Pluto's last enemy and the final boss of the arc. His apperance matches with his personality too, with no features at all. Make Some Noise: Bora's special ability is to create strong soundwaves by shouting his name, blowing away everything in his path. Sadist: He doesn't talk at all, but he gleefully watches Astro Boy in hopes to kill both him and Pluto, giggling a little bit to boot. Super-Strength: Much more stronger than the already titanic Pluto! With 2 million horsepower, Bora was able to not just subdue Pluto, but rip him to pieces.

characters.name: Dr. Abullah
characters.description: Robotic Reveal:He reveals himself as a robot when both Pluto and Bora are destroyed.

characters.name: Cora
characters.description: Boyish Short Hair: She has medium short black hair. Cool Big Sis: She acts as a responsible and reasonable surrogate older sister to the rest of the junk yard orphans. Cute Bruiser: She may be cute but she's also feisty and sassy. Deadpan Snarker: Likes to speak in sarcasm whenever something personal comes up about herself and sometimes in general. Everyone Has Standards: Cora felt betrayed that Astro never told her he's a robot. It's when Hamegg tortures Astro to fight ZOG that she realizes her foster father is crossing the line and is saddened when Astro leaves.

characters.name: Zane
characters.description: Everyone Has Standards: Zane, like the other children, felt indifferent when he learns Astro is a robot. However, when Hamegg relentlessly shocks Astro to force him to fight ZOG, Zane is the first to plead Hamegg to leave him alone by seeing how much agony his friend was in. Never Learned to Read: Implied to be this, as he didn’t understand what Trash Can wrote in the dirt when it tried to tell the orphans that Astro is a robot.

characters.name: Widget and Sludge
characters.description: Half-Identical Twins: Sibling Seniority Squabble: Tears of Fear: Widget, upon seeingthe Peacekeeper/President Stonewhen she, her brother, and the other children were caught by it.

characters.name: President Stone
characters.description: Ambition Is Evil: Stone is desperate to win the election taking place, and by doing so, he has Tenma and Elefun create the Peacekeeper so he could wage war against the surface-dwellers, a threat that only exists in his own mind The Assimilator:Becomes this after the Peacekeeper absorbed him, using its abilities to grow larger and powerful. Big Bad: Of the 2009 movie. Big "NO!": Emits one when Tenma revives Astro Boy and allowed him to escape. Canon Foreigner: He was created for the 2009 movie because Tenma was given a case ofAdaptational Heroismand a new character was created to fill his place.

characters.name: Orrin
characters.description: Brain with a Manual Control: At one point in the movie, his mind was temporarily tampered with by Toby so he can direct him to the Ministry of Science. Nervous Wreck: He is easily scared and screamed when he saw Astro, thinking it was the deceased Toby. Nice Guy: A pretty pleasant guy all around. Robot Butler: Is the domestic robot servant of the Tenma family household.

characters.name: The Peacekeeper
characters.description: A.I. Is a Crapshoot: Elefun warns President Stone that under no circumstances, the red core must be added into the Peacekeeper, as its energy is extremely corrupting and would cause the robot to become both unstable and hostile to everything. Stone,Stupid Evilas he is, ignores this and enforces this trope on the Peacekeeper, leading to Toby's death when he gets caught in a disintegrating ray. The Assimilator: One of the many features that the Peacekeeper has its the "adaptive technology", giving it the ability to assimilates nearby objects for its advantage, as seen when it absorbs twoAttack Dronesinto its body and uses their guns to bring even moreAttack Drone.Once he assimilates with President Stone, thePresident Eviltakes over the Peacekeeper's body, using its adaptive technology to assimilate everything around him, buildings included and growing in both size and strength with it. Arm Cannon: It comes equipped with a twin arm cannons that are devastating in combat. Flat Character: It is nothing more than just being the reason of the creation of Astro and theFinal Bossof the movie.

characters.name: Zog
characters.description: Humongous Mecha: Easily the biggest robot in the film,aside of the Peacekeeper-fused president Stone. Three Laws-Compliant: Defied. When Zog comes to rescue Astro from Hamegg's abuse and is about to kill him, theRepulsive Ringmastertries to convince Zog to not squash him dead, arguing that robots cannot hurt humans. Zog simply responds that he is from old school, before the laws were made. Astro though, saves Hamegg regardless of his recent treatment.

characters.name: Reno
characters.description: An 11-year-old mechanic who used to perform in the Robot Circus with his family. Circus Brat: Was found in a junkyard and raised by robot circus performers at an early age. Gadgeteer Genius: Is great with machines to such a degree that his family helped send him off to formally study robotics, believing continuing a life in the circus would be a waste of his talents. Reused Character Design: Though he has no manga counterpart, he shares an appearance with a previous Tezuka character like many others in his Star System: namely Mamoru from Ambassador Magma. Ship Tease: With Princess Kaaya.

characters.name: Acetylene Lamp
characters.description: A cruel politician who only sees robots as tools and seeks to ban them. Ascended Extra: From one of Tezuka's reoccurring Stars in the manga and a one-off villain in the 1980 series to a major antagonist in the 2003 anime. Corrupt Corporate Executive: A manipulativeJerkasspolitician who is the heir to a robot manufacturing empire. Freudian Excuse: Was once taken care of while stranded on an island by a robot he called Friend. When a volcanic eruption separated them, a helicopter came to pick him up and he was forced to leave behind Friend, who had went into the danger site to get food for him. Unable to cope with the fact that he had abandoned someone he cared about, he convinced himself that robots were tools without hearts. Hypocrite: He proclaims himself as a savior of man who is trying to defend everyone from the supposed threat robots with hearts represent. He ends up causing more damage and put more people at risk of death than robots like Astro did. Inspector Tawashi alsoshuts him upwhen he tries to defend himself that he fought for humankind.

characters.name: Yuko Kisaragi
characters.description: Professor Ochanomizu's hard-working assistant. A Day in the Limelight: The episode "Memories of a Giant" focuses on her past and personal relationship with a robot helper.
